description: Secrets copied into image layers can remain after later deletion.
body:
Never copy secrets into build layers.

Derived from reviewed skill survey outputs, including canonical rules, coverage reports, and trusted skill notes. Paraphrased into domain-pack seed form.

Source rule: this is a generalized, paraphrased seed from local skill survey material; apply only with current repository evidence and more specific pack precedence. Use when Docker Platform work touches environment state, artifacts, permissions, logs, deployment descriptors, and rollback evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include dry-run output, local smoke, artifact checks, config diffs, and rollback notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves external state drift, secret exposure, irreversible changes, and unowned operational impact, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
